title: DEATH WITH DIGNITY ACT - 211info
text: OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION DEATH WITH DIGNITY ACT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A permissive law that allows terminally ill adults to end their lives through the voluntary self-administration of a lethal medication, expressly prescribed by a physician for that purpose. The website offers information on frequently asked questions, forms for patients and providers, and other end-of-life resources. Patients who meet the criteria can request a prescription from a licensed Oregon physician. The physician must be a Doctor of Medicine or Doctor of Osteopathic Medicine licensed to practice medicine by the Oregon Medical Board. 800 NE Oregon Street Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: -Age 18 years and older -Capable of making and communicating health care decisions for him/herself -Diagnosed with a terminal illness that will lead to death within six months. It is up to the attending physician to determine whether these criteria have been met. -Must come to Oregon for the minimum 15-day process and take the life-ending prescription to die in Oregon (no residency requirement) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (971) 673-1201 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (971) 673-1222 Main phone (971) 673-1299 FAX 711 TTY/TTD (800) 422-6012 Toll-free Main phone (971) 673-1201 Center For Health Statistics Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website or call for information. "Reporting forms and instructions" can be found on the named tab on the left side of the website opening page. FEES: Varies by service. Direct costs for participation might include office visits relating to the request, a psychological consult (if required), and the cost of the prescription. DOCUMENTS: Completed Patient Form for attending physician AREA SERVED: Nationwide Services: Specialized Information and Referral * Death and Dying Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - ALERT IMMUNIZATION INFORMATION SYSTEM COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - OREGON SCREENWISE PROGRAM CONSUMER REGULATION - BIRTH CERTIFICATES CONSUMER REGULATION - OREGON VITAL RECORDS CONSUMER REGULATION - SENIOR FARM DIRECT APPLICATION FOR FARMERS & FARM STANDS DISASTER SERVICES - STATE EMERGENCY REGISTRY OF VOLUNTEERS IN OREGON DISASTER SERVICES - WINTER WEATHER ADVISORIES ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AND IMPROVEMENT - CLIMATE CHANGE AND HEALTH FOOD - SENIOR FARM DIRECT NUTRITION PROGRAM FOOD - WIC FARM DIRECT NUTRITION PROGRAM HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - BREAST AND CERVICAL CANCER TREATMENT HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OREGON IMMUNIZATION PROGRAM HUMAN REPRODUCTION - A SAFE PLACE FOR NEWBORNS HUMAN REPRODUCTION - REPRODUCTIVE AND SEXUAL HEALTH HUMAN REPRODUCTION - STATE OF OREGON CONDOM DELIVERY PROGRAM MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - PSILOCYBIN SERVICES PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - OREGON PRESCRIPTION DRUG PROGRAM ARRAYRX PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - OREGON WIC ADMINISTRATION PUBLIC HEALTH - COMMUNICABLE DISEASE INFORMATION PUBLIC HEALTH - ENVIRONMENTAL PUBLIC HEALTH PUBLIC HEALTH - LOCAL PUBLIC HEALTH AUTHORITY DIRECTORY PUBLIC SAFETY - OHA DRINKING WATER DATA ONLINE REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - EARLY HEARING DETECTION AND INTERVENTION SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - OREGON MEDICAL MARIJUANA PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - VACCINES FOR CHILDREN SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - OREGON HOPELINE SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - TOBACCO QUIT LINE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
